rulings.Case Title: JOHN OWEN VS. CYNTHIA WALLACE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 C.C.P. to Judge BRADEN WOODS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 23, 2026, line 31. DEFENDANT CHERYL WALLACE'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 CCP to Judge BRADEN WOODS. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on February 3, 2026. That department handles all real property/landlord-tenant law and motion matters. (SF Local Rule 8.10A1.) This case involves a settlement of a landlord-tenant action and department 501 has heard prior law and motion matters. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DIANA DUARTE ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 23, 2026, line 39. DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLGIES, INC., RAISER, LLC, RAISER-CA, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' (Uber Technologies, Inc; Raiser, LLC; Raiser-CA, LLC) motion to strike is GRANTED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts that defendants' agent/employee Montoya acted with malice, i.e., despicably with a conscious disregard of the safety of others, when he "violently chased" plaintiff and decedents. (FAC, pars. 69, 73, 135; CC 3294(c)(1).) Plaintiff, however, does not plead that those acts were done with the knowledge, direction or ratification of an officer, director, or managing agent of defendants. (Scannell v. County of Riverside (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 596, 614.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: EMPORIUM SF, LLC ET AL VS. MILL VALLEY INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 23, 2026, line 43. DEFENDANTS MILL VALLEY INSURANCE SERVICES, INC., RONALD GUREWITZ'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Mill Valley Insurance Services, Inc. and Ronald Gurewitz's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action (fraud/concealment) and the fourth cause of action (negligent misrepresentation). It is otherwise overruled. The court grants plaintiffs' request for judicial notice of Mill Valley and Gurewitz's cross-complaint. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person.
